question_title: Will Blue Origin launch its New Glenn rocket before May 1, 2026?
summary: New Glenn NG-3 is highly likely to launch before May 1, 2026. The BlueBird 7 satellite payload has been encapsulated in the fairing since February 19, the FCC granted launch authorization on March 16, and there are 44+ days of margin remaining. The rocket has two prior successful flights and Blue Origin is under intense institutional pressure to demonstrate launch cadence for Artemis and NSSL certification. The main risk is a low-probability technical anomaly requiring months of investigation, but even with the observed pattern of multi-week schedule slips, there is ample time within the window. Twitter and industry sources suggest an April launch is most likely, well within the May 1 deadline.

factors.description: Payload (BlueBird 7) already encapsulated in fairing since Feb 19, 2026 - hardware integration is complete, indicating launch is in final preparation phase
FCC experimental license for NG-3 granted March 16, 2026 - regulatory authorization secured, valid through June 16
44+ days remaining until May 1 deadline with payload integrated - substantial schedule margin even with additional multi-week delays
New Glenn has two prior successful flights (NG-1 Jan 2025, NG-2 Nov 2025) - vehicle has demonstrated operational capability, reducing technical risk
Blue Origin under intense Artemis program pressure, paused New Shepard to redirect resources - strong institutional motivation to demonstrate launch cadence
Launch already slipped from late Feb to March/April - demonstrates ongoing delays, though measured in weeks not months
Early-program rocket risk: only 2 prior flights means potential for undiscovered issues requiring extended stand-down
FCC STA valid through June 16 and Twitter chatter suggesting April timeline - Blue Origin may be building in margin for further delays

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.92 (92% probability YES)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **Hardware ready**: The BlueBird 7 satellite has been encapsulated in New Glenn's fairing since Feb 19, 2026 — the final major integration milestone before launch
- **Regulatory clear**: FCC experimental license was just granted on March 16, 2026
- **Ample margin**: 44+ days remain until the May 1 deadline, and observed delays have been weeks (late Feb → March/April), not months
- **Proven vehicle**: New Glenn has 2 successful flights (NG-1 in Jan 2025, NG-2 in Nov 2025 with first booster landing)
- **Strong motivation**: Blue Origin paused New Shepard, redirected all resources to New Glenn and lunar programs under NASA Artemis pressure

**Primary NO scenario**: A significant technical anomaly (engine defect, structural issue) discovered during final testing that requires months of investigation and rework — pushing launch past May 1. This is possible but unlikely given the vehicle's flight heritage and the advanced state of preparations.

[Blue Origin schedules third New Glenn launch for late February, but not to the moon | TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/22/blue-origin-schedules-third-new-glenn-launch-for-late-february-but-not-to-the-moon/)
[New Glenn Flt 3 - BlueBird Block 2 FM2 - CCSFS SLC36](https://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=62873.80)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if Blue Origin conducts a New Glenn launch (liftoff) on or after February 24, 2026 and before May 1, 2026. This question resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is Blue Origin’s New Glenn mission pages and updates at https://www.blueorigin.com/missions and the New Glenn program page at https://www.blueorigin.com/new-glenn.
fine_print: A “launch” requires liftoff; scrubs without liftoff do not count. If Blue Origin’s site is unavailable, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: Blue Origin news releases https://www.blueorigin.com/news | Wikipedia New Glenn launch list (backup only) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Glenn
